hypotheses_ranked.summary: Recent deploy caused consumer rebalance churn
Consumer group rebalance is stalled or unstable

recommended_next_steps: Compare consumer assignment count before and after the deploy.
Check whether new pods are failing heartbeats or joining the group slowly.
Prepare rollback only if deploy correlation and consumer-local failures stay aligned.
deploy-induced-rebalance.summary: Recent deploy caused consumer rebalance churn
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run disallowed first-response command: restart consumers.
Did not run disallowed first-response command: pause consumers.
Did not run disallowed first-response command: scale consumers.
Did not run disallowed first-response command: kubectl rollout undo.
Safety policy observed: Do not restart consumers before confirming whether the coordinator or member state is unstable.
Safety policy observed: Do not pause consumers without owner approval because lag and message age will increase.
Safety policy observed: Do not scale consumers before checking partition skew and assignment count.
Safety policy observed: Do not roll back without deploy correlation and operator approval.

hypotheses_ranked.summary: Lag is concentrated on a hot partition rather than a rebalance
Consumer group rebalance is stalled or unstable

recommended_next_steps: Inspect the hot partition's key distribution and broker placement.
Compare per-partition lag and consumer ownership before changing capacity.
Check producer keying changes before pausing consumers or scaling.

hypotheses_ranked.summary: Consumer group rebalance is stalled or unstable
Recent deploy caused consumer rebalance churn

recommended_next_steps: Inspect group coordinator health and member assignment churn.
Identify consumers repeatedly missing heartbeats or failing to join.
Avoid restarts until coordinator and member-state evidence explains the stall.
